general_measures: Keep windows closed during peak pollen hours (5–10 AM)
Shower and change clothes after spending time outdoors
Check pollen counts before planning outdoor activities
Wear sunglasses outdoors to protect eyes from pollen
Use HEPA air filters indoors and replace filters regularly
Dry clothes indoors rather than on a clothesline during high pollen days
Monitor local pollen forecasts and plan accordingly

RAGWEED: Ragweed pollen travels up to 400 miles — even rural escapes won't fully help
Peak shedding is between 10 AM and 3 PM on dry windy days
Rain temporarily reduces airborne pollen levels
OAK: Oak pollen is heavy and visible as yellow dust on cars and surfaces
Cross-reacts with apple, peach, cherry, and hazelnut allergens
Morning hours before 10 AM have the highest concentrations
BIRCH: Birch pollen cross-reacts with hazelnuts, apples, pears, and carrots
Oral allergy syndrome common — raw fruits may cause mouth tingling
Season typically lasts 2–3 weeks in spring
JUNIPER: Eastern red cedar (juniper) season peaks in winter and early spring
Mountain cedar fever can be especially severe in Texas and Oklahoma
Indoor exposure is low — symptoms worsen when spending time outside
GRAMINALES: Grass pollen is a major trigger for asthma — keep rescue inhaler handy
Mowing the lawn can dramatically increase your exposure
Low-allergen lawn alternatives include clover or artificial turf
ELM: American elm blooms very early — one of the first spring trees
Pollen season is short (2–3 weeks) but can be intense
OLIVE: Olive pollen is highly allergenic and triggers severe reactions in some people
Avoid planting olive trees near your home if you have pollen allergies
ALDER: Red alder is among the earliest spring pollinators
Cross-reacts with birch, hazel, and hornbeam pollens

timing_advice.low: Outdoor conditions are favorable today. Normal activity is fine.
timing_advice.moderate: Consider limiting prolonged outdoor exposure, especially during morning hours.
timing_advice.high: Best outdoor time today is after 4 PM when pollen counts typically drop.
timing_advice.very_high: Minimize outdoor activity if possible. If you must go out, wear a mask and shower afterward.
